text.title: Personas Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the personas methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Personas MUST be derived from ≥6 user interviews; pre-research 'made-up' personas MUST be marked PROTO and MUST NOT drive feature decisions.
rule.rationale: Pre-research personas become self-fulfilling prophecies; teams design to confirm, not discover.

rule.statement: Primary personas MUST be capped at 3-5; secondary personas at <=2. 8+ personas MUST be rejected and re-clustered.
rule.rationale: Beyond 5 primary teams default to 'applies to everyone' and personas become wallpaper.

rule.statement: Clusters MUST be formed on goals + behaviours; demographic-only clusters (age, gender, income) MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Demographics don't predict behaviour; goals + behaviours do.

rule.statement: Every persona MUST include >=1 verbatim quote tied to a research participant ID; paraphrased or LLM-invented quotes MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Quote-with-ID prevents agent fabrication; participant ID enables traceability.

rule.statement: Every persona MUST carry a usage scenario + JTBD pairing ('<name> hires <product> to <job>').
rule.rationale: Personas without scenarios are wallpaper; JTBD makes the persona operational.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
